I'm gonna bear my soul a bit to you guys today about my own struggles I have and I want you to internalize this because what I do always I I have this ability and it's through time and training and and your repetition of forcing myself to use my struggle to use my pain as The greatest motivating factors in my life the biggest mistake people make is they allow their pain They allow their their worries and fears which we all have to disempower them versus to empower them They use them to hold them back from achieving success versus propelling them to success give you an example Nothing to do a business. I had this massive surgery right back on February 14th Valentine's Day, right? coming up now on Almost three months right and two and a half months in and I made this remarkable recovery I mean, it's incredible. I've never had a surgery that's gone so well in terms of the recovery phase or I'm 100% pain-free zero zero pain, right? Except if I push it past the point of like, you know, right my range of motion I've gotten back by 80% plus of my range of motion well on the way to 100% I'm almost ready to start putting weight on it to start doing weight-bearing exercise Which means I can lift weights again. Thank the Lord. So here's the deal for the first two months I was not allowed to do basically anything the first one not even physical therapy, right? Then I started physical therapy was just massaging out the scar tissue, right? And I started noticing something No, that I looked at the mirror. I hate it what I saw Fucking hated what I saw. I looked in there. I looked at my body my fit everything on my arm My god, what is how I'm getting old? Look at my body is no more getting fat and I saw really looking at you know And once you get into that mindset so much you can find anything wrong yourself and magnify it, right? And I got the clearance to start doing your signal going out and walking Biking maybe some lower body exercises for the last month. I could have done it, right? And I couldn't Get myself off the mark for whatever reason it was. I get you know every just I'm gonna start them So I just couldn't get myself off the mark now obviously, you know, I never start with my business I always was able to focus and that's a must have no choice there, right? But for this elective aspect of my life by you know, my how I see how I look how I essentially feel about my body I'll let it go long and I should have and I in the last two weeks. I I Just got so miserable about it. Just how I felt how I look how I felt in my clothes and What was it? I asked you what was it that was holding back? Why did I not get out there to the gym at least get on the bike get on the Stair mast whatever it might be and You know, I'm out. I tell you the narrative in my head. Oh, I hate just doing legs at the gym to me It's the you know, I'll wait till hey, just give me four more weeks where I can start doing weight-weigh bearing on my upper body So I can go to the gym to do a full routine and blah blah blah. That was my story, right? I was telling so it's also nice little cold yet. I can't really go on the beach to do my walks I like all the reasons why but you know what it really was one word Tell you what it is inertia That's what it was and it's what's holding you back from getting what you want in life to inertia meaning Newton's old law like an object in motion tends to stay in motion an Object that's stationary tends to stay stationary until a force is exerted upon it And by the way when a force when the object is in motion The force needed to move it is much less than when you have an object that's stationary to actually get it moving Takes more effort to get something in motion than to push it faster. You get it. There's getting off the mark Getting yourself to just start is hard And I won't do that it could be hard because it's like you use for whatever reason It shouldn't be because what should be so hard about just getting started something because it really should be no easier They just continuing doing something that you're in a routine. You're doing it every day. It's easy, right? But yet getting started those first that you did most the first day first couple of days. This is difficult What is it about? There's something about the physical laws of the universe about this inertia that when you are Stationary when you are not taking a certain action trying to move in a direction. You're not in the first step is the hardest but once you start There's this self perpetuating effect almost like where you know like the pendulum swing where it starts to almost happen automatically Why am I telling you this the reason is because as you see I'm out here walking now I got so fed up. I took a deep breath at those for a whole I said that's it And I drew what I call a line in the sand watch a true line in the sand You see that line that's my line in the sand, right? What was the line the line was I'm not gonna allow myself To go one more day Without taking some action to get myself back in shape Let me the problem was is that it's not just that I've been you know, recovering for two and a half months There was a year before this I couldn't use my arm you get it I used to work that every day. I played tennis all the time I was always ripped in great shape and they taste like when it comes to making money They're just moving through life just having the physical wear with all the energies all that energy You know the energy of your body's gotta you gotta you know be part of it And you know you really realize that when you get sick That's when you realize how important your body is in this whole equation So the point was I had a year where my shoulder was blown out I couldn't play tennis anymore couldn't really exercise and I'm fortunate to have a lot of muscle memory All right, they you know turned to a fat ball But I will tell you that last week I looked at my body. I was like this. I said no I'm drawing my lion's sand right now and that's that I just said I'm not going one more day And I made a commitment. That's the story guys You've got a draw a line in the sand on your own life and say I'm not going one more day Without getting myself moving in the direction. I want to go I don't care whether it's exactly the right direction the road, but you're moving Because once you're moving once you start that ball rolling just by the physical laws of the universe It just gets easier
